items.q: Does fusion music mean one band simply covering songs from another genre?
items.correct: No - fusion blends the actual musical ingredients of traditions, such as Celtic melodies over West African rhythms with dance production.
items.misconception: Fusion is just a rock band playing a reggae song.
items.random_wrong: Fusion means recording two albums at the same time.
items.why: A cover keeps one style; fusion creates a new style by integrating elements from several, which is what listening questions ask students to identify.

items.correct: No - the kora is a 21-string West African harp-lute, plucked to produce flowing melodic and harmonic patterns.
items.misconception: All traditional African instruments are drums, so the kora must be one.
items.random_wrong: The kora is a brass horn played at festivals.
items.why: Equating African music solely with drumming is a common stereotype; the kora's harp-like sound is central to the melodic side of West African tradition.

items.q: Is bossa nova fast, loud carnival music?
items.correct: No - bossa nova is gentle, quiet and intimate, with subtle syncopation, in contrast to the driving percussion of carnival samba.
items.misconception: All Brazilian music sounds like a loud carnival parade.
items.random_wrong: Bossa nova is sung only by large choirs.
items.why: Bossa nova softened samba's rhythms for small ensembles and soft voices; confusing the two styles leads to wrong descriptions in listening exams.

items.q: Afro Celt Sound System's music fuses which traditions?
items.correct: Irish folk, West African music and electronic dance
items.why: It's Irish folk, West African music and electronic dance. “Viennese waltz and opera” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: On 'Samba Em Preludio', Esperanza Spalding sings while playing which instrument?
items.why: Correct answer: Double bass. “Drum kit” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: The kora is best described as what?
items.correct: A West African harp-lute with 21 strings
items.why: The answer examiners want is: A West African harp-lute with 21 strings. “A Brazilian friction drum” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Bossa nova originated in which country?
items.why: Correct answer: Brazil. “Ireland” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
